Football.title: Thomas Tuchel reveals door is ‘open’ for Max Dowman, 16, to play for England at World Cup after consulting Arsenal squad
Football.content:
THOMAS TUCHEL has sensationally revealed the door is “open” for teenager Max Dowman to star at the World Cup – after consulting Arsenal’s first-team stars.

The 16-year-old Gunners midfielder, who became the Premier League’s youngest scorer in the 2-0 win over Everton a week ago, has played just 43 minutes in the top-flight.





Dowman will be part of England‘s under-19s squad next week for a tournament in Portugal while England face Uruguay and Japan in friendlies at Wembley.

Tuchel said: “Why wouldn’t I keep the door open? I keep the door open for anyone with any age. I learned this as a coach, not to take the last decision now.

“It’s very young, yes, and there are arguments that it’s too young but why be open to all these arguments now. Let him fight for his minutes. At the moment he’s not a regular starter, you could argue you should maybe be a starter at your club to be considered.

“Why wouldn’t I rule it out? Of course it’s impressive what he’s doing. It’s impressive the feedback I get from players and people who know him, and we have a look at it and we make the last decision when we build the squad in May.”

Arsenal’s Declan Rice, Bukayo Saka, Eberechi Eze and Noni Madueke all feature in the 35-man squad for the two games – and Tuchel has spoken to all of those players.

Asked whether he thinks Dowman is a game-changer, Tuchel added: “Exactly.”

Controversially, Theo Walcott, then aged 16, went to the 2006 World Cup with England but did not play a minute.

Barcelona superkid Lamine Yamal made his debut for Spain in 2023 aged 16 years and 53 days.



Tuchel added: “There’s also the risk to compare it to Lamine Yamal, Messi and so on, and the age when they have made their way into it. I’m not aware of it but has Lamine Yamal played more minutes for his club before he played for his country? I would say so, yes.

“I hear Max has the best surroundings around him and the best support system around him. He is in a good club where teamwork is rule No 1. So these are all good signs but yes, I would prefer not to talk too much about him because I feel it just doesn’t help.”

The likes of Harry Kane and Declan Rice will be given a rest so will not link up with the squad until after the first game against Uruguay next Friday. Japan are the visitors four days later.

Tuchel also said Liverpool‘s 17-year-winger Rio Ngumoha had an outside chance of making the World Cup squad.



Speaking to 5Live, he said: “Why should I rule that out? I mean, Rio has a good season in Liverpool now and is making his way into the team.

“It’s March and we leave in June, we have to name our squad in late May, so everything is possible.”

Football.title: Top striker dubbed ‘Iranian Messi’ EXPELLED from national team after breaching government’s strict rules
Football.content:
IRAN have reportedly expelled one of their top strikers ahead of the 2026 World Cup.

The player was judged to have acted in a disloyal manner to the government during the ongoing conflict after pictures emerged of him with the leader of Dubai.





Sardar Azmoun, previously described as the Iranian Messi, posted a photo on Instagram of him shaking hands with the leader of Dubai, Mohammed bin Rashid.

Iranian media outlets report that the 31-year-old is unlikely to feature for his country any time soon.

The forward – who has previously been linked with Premier League leaders Arsenal – plays for Shabab Al Ahli in the UAE Pro League.

He was criticised on state television on Thursday night by football pundit Mohammad Misaghi.

He said: “It’s unfortunate that you don’t have enough sense to understand what kind of behaviour is appropriate ⁠at a given time.”

He continued to slam the striker: “We should not mince words with such people. They should be ⁠told that they are not worthy of wearing the national team jersey.

“We have no patience for this sulking and childish behaviour. National team players should be people who proudly belt out the national anthem and deserve to wear the Iran jersey.”

While Azmoun might be out of Iran’s plans – the whole team might be absent at the 2026 World Cup.



This is due to the ongoing conflict with host nation the USA.

President Donald Trump previously said that it would not be “appropriate” for Iran to compete because of reasons surrounding “their own life and safety”.

Mexico has offered to host Iran’s games – but this could cause major issues.

Iran’s football federation are yet to confirm whether Azmoun is definitely expelled from the national team.

However various news sources within the country are indicating that he will be.



Iran are set to face Nigeria and Costa Rica in the upcoming international break.

Football.title: Furious Strasbourg fans coming to London for joint protest alongside Chelsea supporters against BlueCo owners
Football.content:
FURIOUS Strasbourg fans are coming to London to form a joint protest alongside Blues supporters ahead of Chelsea vs Manchester United on April 18.

Chelsea fan group ‘NotAProjectCFC’ announced the move today against the BlueCo ownership after it emerged earlier in the week that they met with Blues director Danny Finkelstein, before making the details of the meeting public.





The group said in a statement: “NotAProjectCFC is proud to announce joint protest action in unison with four supporter groups representing more than 2,000 fans of Racing Club de Strasbourg ahead of Chelsea’s match at home to Manchester United on Saturday, 18 April.

“As a result of the continued erosion of values at both football clubs, we have decided to come together to take action with one clear, unified message: BlueCo Out.

“Members of Ultra Boys 90, Kop Ciel et Blanc, Fédération des supporters du RCS and Pariser section have kindly agreed to fly over to London and walk in unison with us on a protest march towards Stamford Bridge ahead of the match.

“Supporters of both clubs are invited to join us on this march.

“We plan to shine a light on not only the incompetence and mismanagement at Chelsea Football Club, but also the restrictions implemented by multi-club ownership, where clubs like RC Strasbourg are being stripped of their identities, and where longstanding and respected fan groups are being censored and repressed by a brutal ownership.

“We believe this could be a seismic moment in the history of football, where fans of clubs from separate countries will come together to do what is right not only for our clubs individually, but for the sport more widely.

“Together, we can force change. #BluecoOut”

SunSport understands roughly 30 Strasbourg supporters will fly to the UK to take part in the protest as guests, as the pressure on BlueCo from fans heats up in West London and France.



It’s not the first time Chelsea fans have protested against BlueCo.

But the last one was attended by very few fans, many of whom started singing songs about players the current owners actually brought to the club.

The protest did feature banners ridiculing Chelsea supremo Behdad Eghbali and calling for ownership consortium BlueCo to leave the club, with particular focus on majority shareholders Clearlake Capital, the firm co-founded by Eghbali.

As for Strasbourg, their protests have been far more visible and taken more seriously, though there has still been no change in Eastern France.

SunSport travelled to Strasbourg to speak to fans last year, who fear their club is nothing but a “Chelsea B team” and that they are but “slaves eating the master’s leftovers” when it comes to transfers between the two sides.



Their protests have included staying entirely silent at the start of games, and numerous banners being taken into the ground and displayed.

Now, though, there are questions of censorship being raised in the city not far from the German border.

Last month, ahead of a Ligue 1 game at home to Lyon, fans were denied a banner being allowed in the stadium, which read: “Chelsea doesn’t decide what’s going on at Strasbourg”.

One fan described the situation as “severe censorship”, and that security were given “direct instructions from the top”.

On Thursday, in the Conference League, ahead of their game against Rijeka, it happened again.



‘It’s sheer nonsense’

Strasbourg supporter Alexandre told SunSport: “Supporters group ‘Kop ciel et blanc’ wanted to bring a banner simply stating ‘Freedom of speech’ to the stadium, but it was denied in this whole new process of authorisation.

“We know that the instructions about that come from the very top. So instead, inside the stadium, they grabbed a piece of fabric and some paint, and re-did it with the same wording.”

Alexandre added: “It is completely Orwellian that we have to play cat and mouse just to display a piece of fabric about freedom of speech.

“It’s sheer nonsense. We know that the security guards feel the same, but they have orders.”

Meanwhile, a spokesperson for NotAProjectCFC told SunSport of the protest: “Dialogue between both sides has been very productive, and we are unified in our message.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“We want Blueco to leave. We are extremely grateful to fans of RC Strasbourg for making this magnificent effort.

“We feel there is an increasing level of disconnect from both clubs among the fanbases and this is our time to stand up and make our voices heard.”

Football.title: Jose Mourinho pays heartbreaking tribute to ex-Man Utd and Chelsea coach – ‘in my family you’ll stay alive’
Football.content:
JOSE MOURINHO has paid an emotional tribute to his long-time friend and trusted coach Silvino Louro after his tragic passing.

Former Manchester United and Chelsea coach Silvino Louro has passed away at the age of 67.





Mourinho, who worked closely with Louro across multiple clubs, led the tributes with a heartfelt message.

The Portuguese boss said: “In my family you are still alive.

“I will still hear you saying before every game, ‘Bro, it’s gonna be okay.’”

Louro joined Manchester United in 2016 as part of Mourinho’s backroom staff and spent three seasons at Old Trafford.



During that period, United lifted both the Europa League and the League Cup.

David De Gea – Man Utd’s No1 at that time – also paid tribute, sharing a photo alongside his former coach with the simple message: “Rest in Peace.”

Louro had previously built a strong reputation working under Mourinho at Chelsea, Inter Milan and Porto.

He transitioned into coaching in the early 2000s following the end of his playing career.



As a goalkeeper, he represented clubs including Porto, Benfica and Vitoria Setubal.

He also earned 23 caps for Portugal during his career.

Manchester United said: “We are saddened to learn our former goalkeeping coach Silvino Louro has passed away.

“Our thoughts go out to Silvino’s family and friends at this difficult time.”

Chelsea added: “Everyone at Chelsea FC is deeply saddened by the passing of our former goalkeeping coach, Silvino Louro.



“Our thoughts and condolences are with his family and friends at this difficult time.”

Tributes flooded in from across the football world, with fans and former colleagues remembering Louro’s impact on and off the pitch.

One supporter wrote: “One of ours has passed away. May God hold him in His glory.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Another added: “Even today Mourinho spoke about him… Rest in peace Silvino.”

Benfica said he would “forever remain in the memory” of supporters, while Porto highlighted his incredible success both as a player and a coach.

Football.title: Bruno Guimaraes Man Utd transfer talks claims slammed by angry Eddie Howe as Newcastle boss calls it ‘disrespectful’
Football.content:
EDDIE HOWE slammed “nonsense” and “disrespectful” claims Newcastle’s Bruno Guimaraes is in “advanced talks” with Manchester United.

The Toon boss instead insisted that the “totally committed” skipper is doing everything possible to help fire the Mags back into Europe.



A host of top clubs are circling around Howe’s star men with their hopes of qualifying for the Champions League looking slim.



He admits that the transfer noise is “unwanted” ahead of Sunday’s crunch derby with Sunderland.

But he furiously hit back at talk from Guimaraes’ homeland that his Samba ace is lining up a switch to Old Trafford.

Howe said: “Is it a problem for us? I don’t necessarily think it is a problem for us. But it is unwanted news, I suppose, is the best way to put it. You don’t want the players distracted. I don’t know how much they will absorb that.

“For me, it is not the time to think about it. In the summer, you can understand those stories a little bit more because the transfer window is open.

“To say our captain is in discussions with another club is totally disrespectful to Bruno more than anything else, he is totally committed here.”

Howe added: “I’m not going to be impolite, as this isn’t the forum for that, but for me, it is just a nonsense story.

“To say our captain is in discussions with another club is totally disrespectful to Bruno more than anything else, he is totally committed here.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK



“Bruno’s our captain, he’s fully committed, he’s injured at the moment, his only focus is coming back to fitness.”

Guimaraes returned from rehabbing his hamstring injury in Brazil this week to travel with his Newcastle team-mates for the 7-2 Champions League drubbing at Barcelona.

But the derby will come too soon for his return, though Sandro Tonali could be available despite limping off at the Nou Camp with a groin problem.

Howe said: “We’ll wait and see. Hopefully not as bad as maybe first feared when I saw him limping off the pitch.

“We’ll see how he is today and then make a decision closer to the game.”

Football.title: England’s World Cup kit released as Nike ditch controversial feature and include nod to Three Lions’ national anthem
Football.content:
ENGLAND’S new kits for this summer’s World Cup have been unveiled – and one includes a nod to the national anthem.

The home strip is a modern version of the classic white Three Lions outfit, with a navy collar and matching sleeves.





A subtle pattern is visible in the fabric on the authentic version of the shirt.

And printed on the inside of the collar are the words ‘Happy and Glorious’ in a reference to the English national anthem.

Nike has also ditched the purple element of the St George’s flag that was on the back of the previous home shirt.

The symbol divided opinion when the strip was released ahead of Euro 2024 with many deeming it controversial.

However, fans were quick to comment on how much they loved the new kits.

One person said: “That is absolutely class!”

Another commented: “Big fan of both of them.”



A third wrote: “White, simple but classic. Red is a masterpiece.”

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

While a fourth added: “Best shirts in years.”



England revealed the home and away shirts for the first time in a promotional video that was released on Friday evening.

Marcus Rashford, Harry Kane, Jude Bellingham and Jordan Pickford are among the players included.

The video also references England’s road to the USA, Canada and Mexico with the animated image of the ‘Three Lions Inn’ motel.

In addition, the names of two England icons are written onto Hollywood Walk of Fame stars – Kane and Bobby Moore, the only Three Lions captain to ever win the major tournament.

While the home kit took centre stage in the promotional video, the new away kit and goalkeeper strip also featured.



England’s new away kit is a red shirt with navy shorts. The nation’s badge is positioned in the centre, unlike on the home kit where it is on the left side.

And the goalkeeper strip, which will be worn by Pickford this summer, includes a blue patterned shirt.

English rapper, singer-songwriter and producer Mike Skinner provided the voiceover and words for the video.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

He even used a line from the theme tune of popular TV sitcom ‘Only Fools and Horses’ in saying: “Stick that pony in your pocket and fetch the suitcase from the van.”

And there is a reference to the nation’s 1966 World Cup win with a nod to Route 66, one of the most iconic highways in the United States.

Football.title: Newcastle plot transfer for £40million Brazil wonderkid but face fresh tug-of-war with Liverpool
Football.content:
NEWCASTLE could add another Brazilian to their ranks as they weigh-up a £40million swoop for Palmeiras wonderkid Allan.

The toon could face another summer transfer tug-of-war with Liverpool to land the budding star.





Eddie Howe is plotting a huge rebuild of his Newcastle squad this summer and wants to strengthen in a number of positions.

Magpies scouts have been deployed in South America to run the rule over the 21-year-old midfielder.

Fellow Samba stars Bruno Guimaraes and Joelinton have already enjoyed great success at under Howe.

And they could soon have another compatriot to take under their wing should the club launch a summer move.



Though Newcastle face a huge fight to land the highly-rated ace with Palmeiras president Leila Pereira desperate to hang on to their prized asset.

Italian giants Napoli have already had one £34m bid knocked back during the January transfer window but the Serie A side remain keen.

Allan, who still has three years left on his contract, is also on Liverpool’s radar. And that could lead to another battle between the Reds and Mags following last year’s Alexander Isak drama.

Isak eventually joined Liverpool in a British record £130m deal following a protracted saga – after the Reds had already poached Hugo Ekitike from Toon’s clutches.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

Allan is one of a number of young talents identified by the Mags as targets.

Union Saint-Gilloise’s Israeli right winger Anan Khalaili, 21, and Cologne’s left winger Said El Mala, 19, have both also been scouted intensely.

But Newcastle are prepared to splash the cash in order to bring in experienced additions to go alongside the young guns.

Despite forking out £69m on Nick Woltemade and £55m on Yoane Wissa, Howe is also on the lookout for a new striker.

Inter Milan’s Italian international Pio Esposito, 20, could be out of their price range but Tottenham’s Dominic Solanke and Chelsea’s Liam Delap, whom they missed out on last summer, could be ones to watch.



Howe is also known to want to bring in a new goalkeeper with Borussia Dortmund’s Swiss ace Gregor Kobel and Brighton’s Bart Verbruggen top targets.

Aaron Ramsdale, recently preferred to Nick Pope by Howe, is not expected to see his loan from Southampton made permanent.

Newcastle hope to bolster their transfer kitty by offloading Greek international goalie Odysseas Vlachodimos. Sevilla want to keep him long-term with the 31-year-old also keen to turn his loan with the LaLiga club into a full-time switch.

Meanwhile, Newcastle are also among the frontrunners to snap up Bournemouth defender Marcos Senesi once he becomes a free agent in June.

Football.title: Thomas Tuchel names strict condition for Max Dowman to make England's World Cup squad
Football.content:
Thomas Tuchel names strict condition for Max Dowman to make England's World Cup squad

Arsenal midfielder Max Dowman was left out of England's 35-man squad for their upcoming friendlies, but Thomas Tuchel has explained his stance on the 16-year-old

View 2 Images Thomas Tuchel explained his thoughts on Max Dowman (Image: Getty Images)

Thomas Tuchel has sensationally opened the door for Arsenal wonder kid Max Dowman to go to the World Cup.

‌



Dowman made history last weekend after becoming the youngest scorer in Premier League history aged just 16 years and 73 days and is English football’s most exciting prospect.

‌



England boss Tuchel did not pick the teenage star for his 35-man squad for the Wembley friendlies with Uruguay and Japan on March 27 and 31 respectively. But Tuchel made it very clear that he is ready to pick him for the United States this summer after describing him as a “game-changer” but insisted he has to earn more minutes at Arsenal.

‌



Dowman, who created Arsenal’s first goal and the scored the second in their 2-0 win over Everton, has managed just 43 Premier League minutes this season. Tuchel said: “Why wouldn't I keep the door open? I keep the door open for anyone with any age. I learned as a coach not to the take the final decision now.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

England fans pick up on major error in Thomas Tuchel's squad announcement READ MORE :

Kobbie Mainoo and Harry Maguire’s Man Utd futures decided after Michael Carrick intervention READ MORE :

"Of course it's impressive what he's doing. It's impressive the feedback I get from players and people who know him, and we have a look at it and we take the last decision when we build the squad in May.

‌



“There are arguments that it's too young but why open all these arguments now? Let him fight for his minutes. At the moment he's not a regular starter, you could argue you should be maybe a starter at your club to be considered.

“There's also the risk to compare it to Lamine Yamal, Lionel Messi and so on, and the age when they have made their way into it. I'm not aware of it but has Lamine Yamal played more minutes for his club before he played for his country? I would say so, so, yeah.

View 2 Images Dowman made history against Everton with his goal (Image: Getty Images)

‌



HAVE YOUR SAY! Could Max Dowman force his way into England's World Cup squad? Comment below.

"But this is exactly the danger and the risk of all of that, it becomes a crazy hype that does in the end not help. So we have to be careful and responsible. But he is in the best club where teamwork is most important. But he put himself in the spotlight now with this amazing goal against against Everton. He is a fantastic and outstanding talent.

“At the moment, I think he's in a good place to fight for his position and his minutes at Arsenal and we always have the chance to call maybe for the World Cup. It’s the same with young players is to keep their momentum and their level of fearlessness.”

Football.title: Thomas Tuchel picks out Harry Maguire's 'super-strength' and reveals his World Cup chances
Football.content:
Thomas Tuchel picks out Harry Maguire's 'super-strength' and reveals his World Cup chances

Harry Maguire is back in the England squad for friendlies against Uruguay and Japan after impressive performances for Manchester United and Thomas Tuchel has explained his thinking

View 3 Images Harry Maguire is in with a shot of making the World Cup squad (Image: Mark Cosgrove/Getty Images)

Harry Maguire has completed his remarkable career renaissance with an England recall. Manchester United veteran Maguire, 33, has not played for England in nearly two years and was out in the cold at Old Trafford before being restored under Michael Carrick.

‌



But Maguire, such a key part of the last two World Cups, is part of the unprecedented 35-man squad for the two Wembley friendlies with Uruguay and Japan on March 27 and 31 respectively. It is a novel idea by Thomas Tuchel who will give the likes of Harry Kane and Declan Rice the first week off but Maguire will get his chance to showcase his talent as well as being a good tourist.

‌



England boss Tuchel actually pointed towards Maguire’s aerial prowess in both boxes as his “super-strength” for a World Cup played in blistering heat when set-pieces may be all important.

‌



Tuchel said: “Harry’s just played super-solid football, in a bit of a slightly different approach than we play. So it will be very interesting how fast he can also adapt to a different style of defending, to a different style of defensive set-up. And then he just plays very, very solid football on a high level, in a very successful team at the moment. And he has a super-strength in defending the box and attacking in the box. So with set-pieces and crosses.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

England release new 2026 World Cup kits as Nike opt for bold away strip READ MORE :

Kobbie Mainoo and Harry Maguire’s Man Utd futures decided after Michael Carrick intervention READ MORE :

“I just want to get to know him, use the momentum, see how he can adapt to our style of defending. And then see him within the group to make a decision in May, if we take him to the World Cup. Because, like I said, in addition to that he has a super-strength in defending and attacking with his head.

‌



“It's interesting how we're trying to build the group, and I want to arrive with a strong group, and I want to be absolutely certain that we have a group that can enjoy weeks and weeks and weeks together. That we have the energy right, and we have the chemistry right.”

View 3 Images Maguire has not played for England since 2024 (Image: Michael Regan/The FA via Getty Images)

It is a similar story for Maguire’s teammate Kobbie Mainoo - such a key player for England at the last Euros - who was a forgotten man under previous boss Ruben Amorim but is also back in. Tuchel said: “He's part of a huge turnaround of results and energy in the Man United team, I think he's started all matches under Michael Carrick, everyone at the FA, guys who played with him at tournaments are full of praise, so it's time to get to know him.

‌



“I think personally where he is still in a phase where he didn't play so long time, he didn't play football, I think the real strength is about to come.”

United’s players have not actually played as many minutes because they are not in Europe and went out of the domestic cups early on. However, the demands on players from players at Arsenal, Manchester City, Newcastle and Aston Villa has been huge. That explains Tuchel’s decision to name 35 but 11 will not actually report up until the end of next week.

They are: Dean Henderson, Dan Burn, Marc Guehi, Ezri Konsa, Nico O'Reilly, Elliot Anderson, Rice, Morgan Rogers, Anthony Gordon, Kane and Bukayo Saka. They have all played over 3,500 minutes.

‌



View 3 Images Thomas Tuchel has named a 35-man squad for the March internationals (Image: Getty Images)

But that means then at least nine will go home. Tuchel refused to reveal who will leave the camp but says they have been told but he also says any of them can make him change their mind.

The call-ups for Dom Solanke and Dominic Calvert-Lewin are eye-catching while James Garner got the nod as he can play right-back and midfield. Tuchel said: “I would never call it A and B… but these players have at the moment more credit with me than the other players. I think just naming it is more clear. I see the danger.

‌



“There can maybe be disappointment on Saturday but they have enough time now to digest it. Their reaction was all there. They were very happy to come. They get a fully focused, a streamlined four days, and they also get the best support from the coaching staff.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

“I just feel that the clubs are not in a position at the moment to rest the players. I never rested my players for the national team. It’s just a reality and it’s just absolutely normal. They have decisive matches coming up.

‌



“If we had two qualifying matches, we would not rest anyone. I would put our priorities absolutely at the top of the list. I would not gamble on that. Everyone would be in camp and we would play the strongest squad in both qualifying matches.

“But we are qualified and we have friendly matches and that changes and gives us the chance to give the players a mental and a physical rest.”

Football.title: Liverpool hit with Alisson injury blow as replacement decision made
Football.content:
Liverpool hit with Alisson injury blow as replacement decision made for Brighton

Liverpool face Brighton in the Premier League on Saturday lunchtime with Arne Slot set to be without a key player

View 2 Images Liverpool have been hit by a big Alisson injury blow before the Premier League clash against Brighton (Image: (Photo by Simon Stacpoole/Offside/Offside via Getty Images))

Alisson Becker will be absent from Liverpool's Premier League fixture at Brighton and Hove Albion with Giorgi Mamardashvili expected to deputise between the posts. The Brazilian shot-stopper missed the 1-0 Champions League loss at Galatasaray last Tuesday (March 10) due to what was characterised as a minor muscle problem.

‌



However, he made his comeback for the 1-1 Premier League stalemate at home to Tottenham Hotspur on Sunday. He then featured again on Wednesday as Liverpool overturned their first-leg loss to thrash the Turkish outfit 4-0 at Anfield and advance to the Champions League quarter-finals.

‌



Nevertheless, Alisson has been excluded from the Reds' travelling party this weekend after it was deemed too risky to field their first-choice goalkeeper at the Amex Stadium.

‌



Liverpool news: Reds receive major Noa Lang update as star eyes potential return date READ MORE :

This means Mamardashvili is poised to feature in a match that could prove crucial to the reigning champions' aspirations of securing next season's Champions League qualification.

Alisson recently had a 12-month extension activated in his Liverpool deal but physical problems have plagued the goalkeeper in recent seasons.

The previous two complete Premier League campaigns have witnessed him sitting out 20 matches in total, with Caoimhin Kelleher filling in during those periods.

‌



FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Mamardashvili has also appeared five times in the league this term following a muscle complaint for Alisson in September's 1-0 group-stage reverse at Galatasaray in the Champions League. Alisson has reportedly been swapped out for Hugo Souza in Carlo Ancelotti's Brazil squad for the forthcoming March international break.

As confirmed by Slot, Mohamed Salah is also set to miss the game against Brighton after asking to be substituted against Galatasaray with an update provided on Joe Gomez as well.

‌



He said of Salah: "Unusual [he is injured], I think you expect the outcome so he is not available for tomorrow. Good thing for Liverpool is we go to international break, bad news for Egypt, he can't go there.

View 2 Images Mohamed Salah will miss Brighton vs Liverpool through injury (Image: Getty Images)

"We hope with what Mo has shown in the past he can recovery faster than other players. He takes such good care of his body, he can be back earlier than others as history has shown. Only two weeks until we go again."

Article continues below

On Gomez, he added: "Joe wasn't able to come in, we tried to postpone it as often we could [against Gala], maybe a miracle can happen I told him if needed him in extra time. He might be - might be - available tomorrow but not to start."

Alexander Isak, Conor Bradley and Giovanni Leoni are out of the clash, though the former is expected to return after the March international break.

Football.title: Trent Alexander-Arnold 'has no England future' under Thomas Tuchel after pre-World Cup snub
Football.content:
Trent Alexander-Arnold 'has no England future' under Thomas Tuchel after pre-World Cup snub

Trent Alexander-Arnold was omitted from Thomas Tuchel's World Cup warm-up squad to take on Uruguay and Japan later this month, as speculation over his international career spirals

View Image Trent Alexander-Arnold has been left out of Thomas Tuchel's football team (Image: Getty Images)

Trent Alexander-Arnold has been told that he has no future with England, while Thomas Tuchel is at the reins. The German head coach unveiled a mammoth 35-man squad for the March international break, in which the Three Lions will face Uruguay and Japan.

‌



Tuchel made the decision to include five goalkeepers for two matches, including Jason Steele, who currently plays second fiddle to Bart Verbruggen at Brighton, and James Trafford, who has been deputy to Gianluigi Donnarumma at Manchester City.

‌



Elsewhere, Fikayo Tomori was given the nod after a long absence from the England camp, Jude Bellingham was included despite his recent injury troubles, and Danny Welbeck was omitted despite outperforming Dominic Solanke, who was included, for the majority of the season.

‌



England retro football shirt drops to £3.19 in clever Sports Direct deal stack READ MORE :

Max Dowman was also left out of the squad despite his endeavours at Arsenal, although the head coach admitted during a press conference on Friday that matters could change ahead of this summer's tournament in North America.

However, the biggest talking point was Tuchel's decision to omit Alexander-Arnold from his plans, having struggled since his controversial move from Liverpool to Real Madrid ahead of last June's Club World Cup.

Baffled by the decision, Mirror Football released a short-notice Make Football Great Again podcast in reaction to Tuchel's selections on Friday, where Harry Brent, John Cross and Jeremy Cross debated what they took from Tuchel's actions. John explained: "I'd be picking Trent Alexander-Arnold all day long, every day and twice on Sunday.

‌



"I think he's supremely talented. There's an amazing stat that basically he's only played 33 minutes at a World Cup in his career. I mean what a terrible kind of look that is for English football and such a talent. But I think the reality is that here, Tuchel doesn't fancy him, does he?

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

"He really doesn't. You know I looked it up yesterday because obviously I did think that today was going to be a Trent story and it was going to be D-Day for Trent. His last cap came in June 2025. I think it's obvious that he's clearly...Tuchel's not his cup of tea.

‌



"I think you've sort of got to look at it maybe, 'Is he offering enough defensively?' That's the issue here, isn't it? And although Tuchel kind of, you know, sidestepped it, was diplomatic and said, no, it's not over yet, I think he is. I'd think it would be easy to say and wrong to say that, 'Blimey, he's not even made it 35 players and that includes Reece James being out injured. Well that's fine, but what would be the point in picking him if he just doesn't feature in his mindset for the World Cup?

"So, Reece James's injury is almost irrelevant, because yes, he could pick him and Reece James is out. Actually, if Reece-James is coming back and he doesn't fancy the player in him, what's the point of giving him false hope? I do think that that's the points here.

"I think Trent is a big omission, it's a big story. For me, my big takeaway was Harry Maguire's recall. I think that's really fascinating. I think it's a positive story, but there's no doubt about it, the biggest omission if you like, the biggest shock factor story is Trent's omission, without a shadow of a doubt for me."

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

Asked if he thinks Alexander-Arnold's time with England is finished, John added: "Yeah, absolutely. I don't think he's got an England future under Tuchel. It's as simple as that. I think it's not the player, it's the profile of player because basically you cannot, you will not convince me that Djed Spence, who I thought was a brilliant call-out, and basically I love the fact then that he got capped and that he was so kind of rewarded for good form...

"But he's been out of form lately. His form has mirrored Tottenham's form. You know, I've seen him a couple of times recently and he was better on Wednesday night in fairness to him, but I'm surprised that he's kept his place. You can't possibly argue that, basically, that Djed Spence would be above Alexander-Arnold, I think, on talent right now.

‌



"But I do think it's obvious to me that it's not about Trent and personality. It's about the profile of player. He sees him as too attack-minded, doesn't fit into his game plan, doesn't fit into his tactics and formation for what he sees as an effective and efficient defence, and therefore he's left him out.

"I just don't see a way back for Trent, unfortunately. I think he's a real crying shame, by the way, under Thomas Tuchel. That, I think, is glaringly obvious, blindingly obvious."

Ahead of the 2026 World Cup, England will take on Uruguay on Friday, March 27. They will then play Japan on Tuesday, March 31.

Football.title: England release new 2026 World Cup kits as Nike opt for bold away strip
Football.content:
England release new 2026 World Cup kits as Nike opt for bold away strip

England have officially unveiled their new kit for the World Cup in the summer

View 2 Images England have unveiled their home and away kit for the 2026 World Cup. (Image: England)

England has revealed their home and away kits for the forthcoming FIFA World Cup. The words 'Happy and Glorious' feature on the collar of Nike's latest kit partnership, while they will wear red in the newly-released away kit.

‌



The Three Lions are looking to end their sixty years of hurt when they head to North America for the tournament, which kicks off on June 11. After a number of leaks in recent months, England supporters now have confirmation of what they will be wearing for the tournament.

‌



Jordan Pickford, wearing the traditional blue strip with a 2026-twist, features alongside captain Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham, both wearing the home kit. Nottingham Forest's Elliot Anderson models the red away strip alongside Marcus Rashford.

‌



Thomas Tuchel leaves door open for Max Dowman World Cup call-up READ MORE :

England fans pick up on major error in Thomas Tuchel's squad announcement READ MORE :

There is a navy detail on the shoulder seam, and the side inserts are red with a navy outline. While the away kit features the Three Lions badge in the middle. The professional-style kit, worn by the Three Lions stars, will debut Aero-Fit technology, replacing Nike's old Dri-Fit ADV technology.

The kits will be released on Monday, March 23. But they are likely to debut in the forthcoming March international break, where they take on Uruguay and Japan.

In 2018, the Football Association (FA) extended its partnership with Nike, keeping the American brand as England's kit provider until 2030. The deal was worth more than £400 million, and the latest kit could be one of the last kits manufactured by Nike with the European Championships to come in 2028, hosted across England and the United Kingdom, before the 2030 World Cup in Africa.

‌



The introduction of the Aero-FIT technology is viewed as the pinnacle of cooling technology, enabling players to perform at their peak in extreme conditions.

Buy the latest England kits and merch This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more Various Prices England Official Store Buy Now on England Official Store Product Description England fans can pick up the latest Three Lions kits and merchandise at the England Official Store.

The Three Lions will take on Croatia, Ghana and Panama in Group L, but they will have to contend with summer weather in Dallas, Boston and New York. But the seeding system has handed them an advantage as they cannot meet Spain or Argentina until the semi-finals or France in the final, should they all make it that far.

‌



It has been a busy Friday at St George's Park after Thomas Tuchel announced his 35-man squad for the forthcoming international friendlies against Uruguay and Japan.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

There was no room for Real Madrid defender Trent Alexander-Arnold, while James Garner and Jason Steele received their maiden call-ups and a return to the fold for Leeds United talisman Dominic Calvert-Lewin.

‌



Ollie Watkins and Alex Scott were among the notable absentees that were omitted from the expanded squad, but Chelsea duo Trevoh Chalobah and Reece James both missed out due to injury.

The German head coach said: "My general thoughts are March is like a bit of an odd one, like the June one.

‌



"It is in a period where players are heavily involved in cup semi-finals, Champions League semi-finals and at the end of the season, they fight for not being relegated or making international or European places, fighting for championships and for titles, so it is a very stressful and very demanding period of time.

"We have obviously done our homework, we have qualified, so it leaves us with two friendlies which is a good thing. The opponents are very strong opponents, given the style of play and given the ranking. It has its demands, this window.

View 2 Images England players celebrate Bukayo Saka's goal against Serbia (Image: Eddie Keogh/The FA)

Article continues below

"We decided then to divide it in basically two camps almost, so we bring players in that we haven’t seen that haven’t played so much to open up the picture and to open up the competition for plane tickets to the US.

"Then from Friday/Saturday, a group of players will come into camp – ten or eleven players will come who get a rest before and we will then go with a new group of players and a new mix of players into the match against Japan."

Football.title: Manager who held talks over Man Utd interim role announces imminent exit
Football.content:
Manager who held talks over Man Utd interim role announces imminent exit

Manchester United have a history of appointing interim managers, with Michael Carrick on his second short-term spell in charge of the club, and they have previously cast the net wide

View 2 Images Ernesto Valverde will leave Athletic Club at the end of the season (Image: Alex Caparros/Getty Images)

Ernesto Valverde has announced that he will leave Athletic Bilbao at the end of the season. Valverde is in his third spell in charge of Athletic and has made the decision to step down once the 2025/26 campaign draws to a close.

‌



Athletic have endured a difficult season and sit 10th in La Liga with 10 league games remaining and Valverde has therefore called time on his role. Despite the difficult spell, the 62-year-old will remain a club legend at Athletic, where he is six games away from reaching 500 in charge.

‌



Valverde has mainly managed in his native Spain with Athletic, Villarreal, Espanyol, Valencia and Barcelona, but also had two spells in Greece with Olympiacos. However, he was one of the candidates shortlisted by Manchester United to become interim manager after Ole Gunnar Solskjaer was sacked in November 2021.

‌



United got in touch with the Spaniard, who was without a job after leaving Barcelona in 2020. He was considered alongside Mauricio Pochettino, who was at Paris Saint-Germain, while Carrick was embarking on his first caretaker spell with United.

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Kobbie Mainoo and Harry Maguire’s Man Utd futures decided after Michael Carrick intervention READ MORE :

In the end, United appointed Ralf Rangnick on a contract until the end of the 2021/22 season. The Austrian won just 11 of his 29 games in charge and was replaced by Erik ten Hag in the summer.

‌



Carrick returned to United as interim boss in January following the sacking of Ruben Amorim and is now in the frame for the full-time job this summer, having won seven of his nine games in charge.

Valverde, meanwhile, is unlikely to be considered this time around, yet will leave Athletic with his head held high, having won the Copa del Rey in 2024 - the club's first major trophy in four decades.

View 2 Images Ernesto Valverde was in the running for the Manchester United job after leaving Barcelona (Image: SIPA USA/PA Images)

‌



“Hello everyone," he said in a video message on Friday. "I’m here today to announce that I won’t be Athletic’s head coach next season. This is a decision I’ve come to over time, one that I’ve discussed with the club, and I wanted to share it with you.

“At the same time, it’s worth noting that we have ten crucial league matches left, starting this Sunday against Betis. Ten matches in which we aim to achieve our goals, in which we have a lot to gain, in which we’re going to give it our all and which, without a doubt, we can win if we all stick together.”

Valverde held talks with United chiefs in November 2021, with him initially battling against Pochettino and Ten Hag for the job. But none of those candidates ended up arriving, with United turning to Rangnick in a left-field appointment, before Ten Hag eventually arrived from Ajax the following summer.

Football.title: Premier League's Champions League qualification explained if Aston Villa win the Europa League
Football.content:
Premier League's Champions League qualification explained if Aston Villa win the Europa League

Aston Villa could qualify for the Champions League through Europa League victory or Premier League finish, with their success potentially opening up extra spots for English clubs

View 2 Images Unai Emery’s Aston Villa are contenders to win the Europa League (Image: Getty Images)

Aston Villa's sole tangible prospect of silverware this season lies with the Europa League, which could also serve as another route to the Champions League for the following season. Villa are battling on two fronts, where the reward for both is a place in Europe's premier cup competition next term.

‌



Unai Emery's squad slipped to fourth position in the Premier League table last weekend following their loss to Manchester United, and are merely two points ahead of fifth-placed Liverpool and three clear of sixth-placed Chelsea. Villa also have the Europa League to anticipate after the international break, having triumphed over Lille 3-0 on aggregate in their last-16 tie this week, which saw Emery and his team secure a spot in the quarter-final, where they will face Serie A side Bologna.

‌



With Villa competing on multiple fronts at the crucial stage of the season, it appears that the outcome of the campaign could influence the rest of the Premier League. As Tottenham did last season, if Villa were to clinch the Europa League, they would automatically qualify for next season's Champions League as a reward for their success.

‌



Man Utd star Jadon Sancho to be offered shock lifeline after boss changes mind on transfer READ MORE :

Premier League clubs face sacking manager nightmare with major law change READ MORE :

What might complicate matters is if Villa win the Europa League and also qualify for the Champions League thanks to their standing in the Premier League table.

A top-four finish in the Premier League would be sufficient for a club to qualify for the Champions League, whilst fifth will almost certainly be enough with England poised to receive an extra Champions League spot via UEFA's coefficient rankings.

Consequently, Villa finishing in the top four or five in the Premier League and winning the Europa League could see the scenario change for the Champions League qualification picture.

‌



View 2 Images Aston Villa will face Bologna in the quarter-finals of Europa League (Image: Getty Images)

If Villa were to finish fifth in the Premier League and win the Europa League, then another spot could be awarded to English clubs for the Champions League.

Villa finishing fifth would see all of the top five qualify for the Champions League, whilst sixth could also earn a spot in the competition thanks to the extra place being given to England for their coefficient ranking.

‌



However, if Villa were to finish fourth and win the Europa League, then England would not receive a bonus Champions League qualification spot.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Then, it would either be the top four or five clubs that qualify, depending on the UEFA coefficient rankings. Liverpool also have a role to play thanks to their participation in the Champions League.

Article continues below

If the Anfield outfit was to win the trophy, Villa win the Europa League, and both clubs occupy fifth and sixth place in the Premier League, then seventh place in the table would then become sufficient to qualify for the Champions League.

Football.title: Luke Littler applies to trademark his FACE as darts star takes steps to protect fortune
Football.content:
Luke Littler applies to trademark his FACE as darts star takes steps to protect fortune

Luke 'The Nuke' Littler is already a global star after the two-time world champion emerged as one of the most-exciting Darts players in years

View 2 Images Luke Littler has filed an application to trademark his face. (Image: Getty Images)

Darts sensation Luke Littler has made a copyright application in a bid to avoid artificial intelligence (AI) fakes. The 19-year-old world number one is among the hottest sports stars in the world after his astronomic rise as a 16-year-old to becoming a two-time world champion as a teenager.

‌



However, he has lodged an application with the Intellectual Property Office (IPO) to prevent AI-powered products using his picture without permission and breaking copyright laws. Littler joins the list of sports stars to file with the IPO in recent years, Chelsea's Cole Palmer copyrighting his 'Cold' celebration.

‌



The Warrington native is the standout star within the Professional Darts Corporation's (PDC) ranks and has become highly marketable. He has released a string of products from Darts-related memorabilia to computer games but this latest move will restrict the murky world of AI fakes.

‌



Luke Littler gets telling off by Sky Sports pundit moments after beating Gerwyn Price READ MORE :

Already, 'The Nuke' has been trademarked in the United States with the 19-year-old Manchester United fan already enjoying the dizzying heights of his success.

Last week, he was spotted at the Cheltenham Festival horse-racing meeting where he was spotted with friends and his girlfriend, Faith Miller.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



In applying to the IPO, Littler has formally requested legal protection for creative work or inventions, including trademarks. It will grant him legal ownership and allow him the opportunity to take legal action if there is a breach of the law.

The news comes less than 24 hours after a dramatic win in Dublin to move second in the Premier League Darts table. Littler won night seven of the competition and had an action-packed night in the Irish capital.

In his win over Michael van Gerwen, he hit a 170 checkout before coming from 5-0 down to beat Gerwyn Price 6-5 in the final.

‌



View 2 Images Luke Littler came back from 5-0 down to beat Gerwyn Price in the BetMGM Premier League in Dublin (Image: PA)

He said: "I've definitely learnt a lot, especially with the fans. In the first game against (Stephen) Bunting I didn't give anything to the fans, I didn't give any reaction and got the job done.

"It was the same in the final when I was 5-0 down, everyone doubted me, I definitely doubted myself but I had a little laugh and a joke. I was just having a bit of fun because I knew I was beaten.

Article continues below

"I'm still learning not to react to the fans. I didn't do much then, only for the first leg, and then I can build on it. It is what it is, people want to see new winners but I've won again."

He will be looking to chase down leader Jonny Clayton on night eight in Berlin, where he begins his mini-tournament against Bunting.
